title: oenothera longissima subsp. clutei
intro: Oenothera longissima subsp. clutei is a subspecies of the evening primrose, a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its high content of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a polyunsaturated fatty acid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oenothera longissima subsp. clutei include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopause, eczema, and rheumatoid arthritis, as well as its ability to support skin health and immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
